judgment.summary: Rapidly growing, highly profitable software company with strong cash generation, though trading at a premium valuation.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimate by 18.1%; next earnings date 2026-08-03.
positives: Revenue growth 84.7% YoY and earnings growth 325.0% YoY indicate exceptional expansion.
Operating margin 46.2% and profit margin 43.7% demonstrate strong profitability.
Free cash flow $1.75B TTM and $8.03B total cash provide significant financial flexibility.
Return on equity 32.6% and return on assets 14.7% show efficient use of capital.
negatives: Trailing P/E 153.87 and forward P/E 66.02 indicate a very high valuation.
Price/Sales (TTM) 62.84 is also exceptionally high, suggesting high market expectations.
Debt/Equity ratio of 2.48, while offset by strong cash, warrants monitoring.

news.paragraph: Jim Cramer recently described Palantir as an "incredibly good company" whose stock has run ahead of itself, suggesting a potential disconnect between fundamentals and current valuation. While the company is praised for its quality, the commentary implies that recent price appreciation may have outpaced underlying business growth. Traders should consider this sentiment alongside the upcoming earnings report as a potential catalyst.

headlines.title: Jim Cramer on Palantir: “Incredibly Good Company Whose Stock Got Ahead”

examples.body: $PLTR Sneaky retail bears always short AH.
$PLTR I'll huff and I'll puff until I smash that wall down!! Bears ain't gonna win
$PLTR This stock has gone nowhere in six months, I mean thats not a short period of time.
$PLTR rejected 20ema on e again and now it’s just hanging around.
